releases.headline: Upload content validation and per-IP storage quota

items.title: Magic-byte content validation on upload
items.body: Uploaded files are now verified against their claimed extension using file-header signatures across 15 formats: PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP, BMP, ICO, TIFF, PDF, ZIP, RAR, 7Z, GZ, BZ2, OLE2 (legacy Office), and SVG. A `.jpg` file containing an executable payload is rejected. OOXML formats (`.docx` / `.xlsx` / `.pptx`) are correctly accepted as ZIP containers, and text formats (`.txt`, `.csv`, `.rtf`) skip validation since they have no reliable magic bytes.

items.title: Per-IP storage quota for anonymous uploads
items.body: New `publicIpQuotaBytes` setting (Admin > Uploads tab, default disabled) caps the total bytes an anonymous IP may upload within a 24-hour rolling window. Prevents a single source IP from consuming unlimited disk space through public drops. Logged-in users continue to use the existing per-user quota.

items.title: Admin logo upload reuses the shared content-type detector
items.body: Refactored the admin logo upload path to call the shared `detectContentType()` from the upload validator instead of carrying a local duplicate of the magic-byte table.

releases.headline: Vault consistency on ML-KEM-1024 and public-share isolation
releases.summary: Vault enable and vault rotation now both use ML-KEM-1024 with 64-byte FIPS 203 seeds. Vault-encrypted files are no longer reachable through public bundle share routes.

items.title: Vault files blocked from public share routes
items.body: `lookupFile()` now rejects vault-encrypted files, preventing unauthenticated access to vault ciphertext via `/s/:shareId/download`. Vault files are reachable only through authenticated `/vault/` routes.

items.title: Admin CSV export omits vault file metadata
items.body: Vault file filenames and sizes are no longer included in the admin file export, since the operator should not be able to enumerate vault contents from the admin surface.

items.title: Vault enable upgraded to ML-KEM-1024
items.body: The dashboard `enableVault()` flow now uses ML-KEM-1024 to match the rotation flow and FIPS 203. Previously it used ML-KEM-768, leaving enable and rotation on different algorithms.

items.title: Vault enable generates 64-byte seeds
items.body: Seed generation now produces the 64-byte `d||z` form required by FIPS 203. Previously generated 32 bytes with zero-padding, mismatching the rotation flow.

items.title: Stale crypto labels in the UI
items.body: Updated all UI references from "ML-KEM-768" to "ML-KEM-1024" and from "AES-256-GCM" to "XChaCha20-Poly1305" across the dashboard, upload page, vault share page, and route comments.

items.title: Compatibility notes for existing vaults
items.body: Existing vaults created with ML-KEM-768 continue to work — `decryptFile()` auto-detects the algorithm by encapsulated key size, and `unlockPasskeyGated()` derives both ML-KEM-768 and ML-KEM-1024 keys. Vault files remain intentionally separate from bundles: they cannot be organized into bundles or accessed via bundle share links, since only the vault owner holds the decryption key.

releases.headline: Security hardening across API keys, SSRF, webhooks, and CORS

items.title: API key scope enforcement on upload routes
items.body: `requireScope()` is now wired into `/drop/init`, `/drop/file`, `/drop/chunk`, and `/drop/finalize`. API keys without the `upload` scope are rejected at the route boundary.

items.title: API key permission validation
items.body: Key creation now validates permissions against the canonical scope enum (`admin`, `upload`, `read`, `webhook`). Arbitrary permission strings are rejected at issuance time.

items.title: SSRF coverage for the full CGNAT range
items.body: Replaced the incomplete string-prefix match for RFC 6598 (100.64.0.0/10) with a proper numeric range check. The previous logic missed 100.80.x.x through 100.127.x.x, leaving part of the CGNAT block reachable from outbound requests.

items.title: Webhook DNS pinning
items.body: The IP resolved during SSRF validation is now pinned via the `lookup` option on `https.request()`, closing the DNS TOCTOU / rebinding window between the validation check and the outbound connection.

items.title: CORS wildcard no longer paired with credentials
items.body: A wildcard (`*`) `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` no longer emits `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true`. Credentialed CORS now requires explicitly listed origins, matching the Fetch spec.

items.title: DB backup serves the encrypted-at-rest file
items.body: The backup endpoint now serves the `.db.enc` file when present, instead of looking for a plaintext `.db` that does not exist in encrypted-at-rest deployments.

items.title: Origin policy warning when `rpOrigin` is unconfigured
items.body: A security warning is now logged when `rpOrigin` is not configured and the policy falls back to localhost in production, so operators notice the misconfiguration before a passkey verification fails.

releases.headline: Vault rotation fixes and landing-page polish

items.title: Vault passkey rotation
items.body: Three bugs prevented vault key rotation from completing. The WebAuthn assertion was encoded with standard base64 rather than base64url (server-side verification failed); ML-KEM seed generation produced 32 bytes instead of the 64 bytes required by FIPS 203; and server-side validation rejected the corrected 64-byte seeds.

items.title: Vault file download after rotation
items.body: Existing vault files could not be decrypted because `expandSeed` used SHA-512 instead of zero-padding, which mismatched the original key derivation used by the enable flow.

items.title: README share-ID bit length
items.body: Documentation incorrectly described share IDs as 128-bit. The code generates 32-byte SHA3-derived IDs (256-bit) — README now reflects that.

items.title: README logo URL
items.body: Switched the logo reference to an absolute `raw.githubusercontent.com` URL so it renders correctly from mirrors and package pages.

items.title: Ko-fi donation link
items.body: Added a Ko-fi link in the README "A final note" section and registered it as the GitHub Sponsors / funding button on the repository.

items.title: Maintainer support setting
items.body: New opt-in admin toggle (Branding tab, off by default) that surfaces a "Support HermitStash" link in the navbar, landing page, and footer with animated gradient text.

items.title: GitHub button on landing-page hero
items.body: Landing page hero now includes a GitHub call-to-action with a shimmer animation alongside the existing primary action.

items.title: Landing-page tagline line break
items.body: Inserted a line break between "Stash it quietly." and "Share it instantly." so the tagline reads as two lines on all viewports.

items.title: Repository hygiene
items.body: Disabled Wiki and Projects on the GitHub repository, added 10 discoverability topics, and enabled Dependabot vulnerability alerts.

releases.headline: Initial release — post-quantum encrypted self-hosted file sharing
releases.summary: First public release of HermitStash, a self-hosted file-sharing server with post-quantum cryptography from the ground up and zero npm runtime dependencies.

items.title: ML-KEM-1024 + P-384 hybrid key encapsulation
items.body: Hybrid KEM combining FIPS 203 ML-KEM-1024 with NIST P-384 ECDH, paired with XChaCha20-Poly1305 for symmetric encryption of all stored data.

items.title: Zero plaintext on disk
items.body: All database fields are vault-sealed; IP addresses are stored as SHA3 hashes. Only counters, status enums, timestamps, and FK IDs remain raw.

items.title: Zero npm runtime dependencies
items.body: All cryptographic primitives are vendored into the repository. No package downloads are required to run the server.

items.title: Passkey / WebAuthn login with PRF-based vault
items.body: Zero-knowledge vault keyed by the WebAuthn PRF extension. The server never sees the vault encryption key in plaintext.

items.title: Chunked uploads with pause / resume
items.body: Large transfers can be paused and resumed; the drop UI supports folder drag-and-drop for whole-tree uploads.

items.title: Teams, audit log, webhooks, and API keys
items.body: Operator surface includes multi-user team management, an immutable audit log, outbound webhook delivery, and scoped API key issuance.

items.title: One-command deploy
items.body: Run `node server.js` directly or use the published Docker image. No build pipeline, no compilation, no external service dependencies.
